codeReview.summary: The commit correctly introduces a 3-tab layout and preserves the expected section grouping, but it misses the explicit requirement to keep transaction history above the tabs with global visibility.

issues.description: The feature contract requires `TxHistoryList` to stay above the tabs for global visibility, but the app renders `TabNav` immediately after the header and never mounts a shared transaction-history component there. Transaction history remains buried inside individual write forms instead, so users lose access to completed TX history as soon as they leave the Lend & Borrow tab.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-worker` skill requires a manual Vite/UI verification step, but this run recorded only static analysis/build commands and still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`. The skill or review conventions should make manual-check evidence explicit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 requires starting Vite and manually checking the rendered UI. The transcript skeleton for worker session `c6243e88-e696-4bba-99ca-3666207a3537` shows no dev-server launch or browser interaction before `EndFeatureRun`, while the handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true.

summary: Fail: the tabbed-layout refactor mostly matches the requested tab structure, but commit `d0588c4` does not preserve a globally visible `TxHistoryList` above the tabs, which was part of the feature definition.
